title: bursera glabrifolia
intro: Bursera glabrifolia, also known as the 'Gum Boil Tree', is a medicinal plant species native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been used for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Bursera glabrifolia include treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use in wound healing and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant include extracts used in traditional medicine, and it can be consumed as a tea or applied topically as an oil or ointment.
